nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.label_negative: Manual lesson planning without AI assistance
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI mostly to help get ideas for lesson plans, generate lists of examples of grammar and language points, and to generate games

nodes.label: AI as real-time classroom adaptation tool
nodes.label_negative: Inability to adapt quickly without AI
nodes.grounding_span: it's great for the on the go nature of teaching and being flexible

nodes.label: AI output quality and appropriateness
nodes.grounding_span: they come out a bit awkward and i still have to tweak them

nodes.label: Preservation of core teaching expertise
nodes.grounding_span: i want to make sure that I thoroughly understand the grammar that i teach, and that i'm not relying on constantly looking things up or asking AI to expain it for me

nodes.label: Collaborative vs directive AI interaction
nodes.grounding_span: if i need ideas for a lesson and i don't really know where I'm starting then it is definitely more collaborative. other times i know exactly what I want and ask the AI to do it

nodes.label: Student engagement and learning
nodes.grounding_span: it worked great. it really helped me out when i was in a tight spot
